title: podman-compose error "pod already exists" after cold reboot
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
Had been using podman-compose successfully for 1month when laptop froze and I had to cold reboot. After that no success initiating.

Exactly same configuration on desktop computer has no problems.

Have tried:
- full reinstall of podman & podman-compose
- `rm -Rf ~/.local/share/container`
- `podman rm -avf` (needed manual input b/c there was a "infra container". After this checked if any instance of pods, volumes or images => none 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman-compose up` in directory having docker-compose.yml

**Describe the results you received:**
```Error: unable to create pod: error adding pod to state: name dev-database is in use: pod already exists
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/david/.local/bin/podman-compose", line 10, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 1093, in main
    podman_compose.run()
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 625, in run
    cmd(self, args)
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 782, in wrapped
    return func(*args, **kw)
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 898, in compose_up
    detached=args.detach, podman_command=podman_command)
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 457, in container_to_args
    mount_args = mount_desc_to_args(compose, volume, cnt['_service'], cnt['name'])
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 386, in mount_desc_to_args
    mount_desc = mount_dict_vol_to_bind(compose, fix_mount_dict(mount_desc, proj_name, srv_name))
  File "/home/david/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/podman_compose.py", line 366, in mount_dict_vol_to_bind
    src = json.loads(out)[0]["mountPoint"]
KeyError: 'mountPoint'
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
working services

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
no

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman pod create --name=dev-database --share net
125
podman volume inspect dev-database_mysql || podman volume create dev-database_mysql
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Also notice

> podman volume ls
```
DRIVER   VOLUME NAME
local    dev-database_mysql
```

> podman volume prune
```
WARNING! This will remove all volumes not used by at least one container.
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
Error: error freeing lock for volume dev-database_mysql: no such file or directory
```

comments.body: isnt this a podman-compose issue which is not part of this repo?
I honestly don't know... the errors come from the script yes but I'm guessing there's some locked entity in cache which libpod cannot modify.

This is a **podman-compose** issue. I had the same problem. I fixed it with a newer version of podman-compose from devel branch of upstream project.
```
pip3 install https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/archive/devel.tar.gz
```

Can confirm that solves the error above!

Still some issues that weren't previously but I think config related.
